response: Implemented in May 2018, the EU’s General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) mandates strict data handling rules for any organization processing EU residents' information, granting individuals rights to access, erasure, and explicit consent while requiring 72-hour breach notifications. Noncompliance carries severe financial penalties of up to 4% of global annual revenue or €20 million, with major fines already issued to Meta (€1.2 billion), Amazon (€746 million), and Google (over €200 million). Despite criticisms regarding heavy compliance burdens and inconsistent enforcement, the GDPR has successfully established data privacy as a fundamental right and inspired similar legislation worldwide, cementing its status as the global benchmark for data protection.
